findings.evidence: `code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/Basic.lean:205` defines `map_reindex_pathLaw`; `code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/PathSpace/Shift.lean:76` defines `map_shift_iterate_pathLaw`; the PR repeats this at `ThreeCycle.lean:97` via `rw [pathLaw_def, Measure.map_map measurable_shift hP, ...]`.
findings.fix: Replace the `pathLaw_def, Measure.map_map ...` part with `map_reindex_pathLaw threeCycleMeasure (fun _ => Measurable.of_discrete.aemeasurable) (fun k => k + 1)` (or import/use `map_shift_iterate_pathLaw ... 1`), then only prove the resulting shifted process law equals the original by `map_add_right_eq_self`.
findings.issue: `threeCycle_measurePreserving_shift` manually proves the map-of-shift/path-law reindexing step with `pathLaw_def` and `Measure.map_map`, duplicating the existing `map_reindex_pathLaw`/`map_shift_iterate_pathLaw` API.

summary: The example is new, but the stationarity proof re-derives existing path-law reindexing API instead of using it.
